mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) indicates healthy consumer spending, sustaining corporate earnings and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns. This trend-driven signal is always active across economic regimes.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or periods with declining RSAFS produce positive 20-day returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A substantial increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE rise >0.3 percentage points over 3 months) signals labor market deterioration, reducing consumer income and spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If after UNRATE trigger the SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered cases, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the real federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation) exceeds 2%, monetary policy is restrictive, compressing equity valuations via higher discount rates and predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal depends on the interaction of the two series.
failure_condition: If episodes with real rate >2% are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle or contractionary regimes, a month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) exceeding 0.5% signals acute supply-side weakness that quickly translates into negative market sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO decline triggers positive 5-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST > 10% drop) indicates a severe demand shock in the housing sector, which historically foreshadows broader economic weakness and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If a >10% MoM decline in HOUST is followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained expansion in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, consistently driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon across all regimes.
failure_condition: If periods of rising PAYEMS are not associated with positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 60% of observations over any 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment (FEDFUNDS above 5%), a sequence of rate cuts (cumulative decrease of 25bp or more within a month) signals emergency monetary easing in response to economic distress, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as markets price in recession risk.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 5% and has decreased by at least 25bp in the last month
failure_condition: If after such easing episodes the SPX produces positive 20-day returns more than 50% of the time, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth (INDPRO 6-month) is robust (>2%) but employment growth (PAYEMS 6-month) is weak (<1%), the divergence signals a late-cycle dynamic where productivity gains mask labor market deterioration, predicting a downturn and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The interaction of proxies is essential.
failure_condition: If the divergence signal is followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, the hypothesis is rejected.
